description: Adela Varick Demo — End-to-end governed SOP lifecycle. Ingest documents into knowledge graph, detect SOP gaps via agent signals, propose governed patches with traceability, approve/reject with versioning. Includes KG visualization, SOP diff page, live SOP test, and benchmark page. Demo date: March 13, 2026.

techStack.backend: Python FastAPI sidecar (adela-graphiti-sidecar/) with Graphiti + Kuzu knowledge graph
techStack.database: Convex (real-time DB, mutations, queries, vector search)
techStack.ai: Anthropic Claude (patch generation, live SOP test), OpenAI text-embedding-3-small (embeddings)
techStack.auth: Clerk (org-based, three roles: vendor_admin, vendor_fde, customer_user)
techStack.graph: Graphiti + Kuzu (embedded graph DB) with 19 entity types and 21 edge types

designSystem.aesthetic: Dark editorial sophistication — serif headlines, monospace labels, sage green accents on deep black.

demoScenario.failure: Two co-signatories (Meridian Capital Group LLC + Meridian Capital Advisors Inc.) not covered by SOP Rule 3.2
demoScenario.governance: G-006 requires explicit multi-entity SOP coverage
demoScenario.patch: Rule 3.2.1 added; SOP versions v2.1 → v2.2

userStories.title: Confirm and add missing entity types to sidecar
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need to verify that entity_types.py has SOPEntity, GovernanceRuleEntity, and AgentSignalEntity, and add any that are missing, so the knowledge graph can classify demo documents correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Check adela-graphiti-sidecar/app/models/entity_types.py for SOPEntity or equivalent
Check for GovernanceRuleEntity or equivalent
Check for AgentSignalEntity or equivalent
Add any missing types as Pydantic models following existing pattern
Add new types to the ENTITY_TYPES dict
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: Do NOT create new infrastructure. Just add Pydantic models to the existing file if missing. Follow the pattern of existing entity types.

userStories.title: Add typed document ingestion endpoint to sidecar
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need a POST /ingest/document endpoint that accepts governance, sop, and agent_log document types and ingests them into the Graphiti knowledge graph.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /ingest/document endpoint exists in adela-graphiti-sidecar
Accepts JSON body with agent_id, document_type (governance|sop|agent_log), content (markdown text), source_name, optional metadata
Calls graphiti.add_episode() with ENTITY_TYPES and EDGE_TYPES
Episode name uses format: {document_type}:{source_name}
Returns JSON with episode_id, entities_extracted, edges_extracted, status (ok|error), optional error message
Does not throw on valid markdown input
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: Add to existing router or create ingest_demo.py in adela-graphiti-sidecar/app/routers/. The three demo documents: governance_rules.md (governance), deal_qualification_sop_v2.1.md (sop), Agent_Log_Mar_11_2024.md (agent_log).

userStories.title: Add gap detection endpoint to sidecar
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need a POST /gaps/detect endpoint that uses the knowledge graph to identify SOP gaps from agent signals.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /gaps/detect endpoint exists
Accepts JSON with agent_id and signal object (triggered_rule, conflict_description, governance_ref, escalation_id, deal_id)
Constructs search query from signal fields and calls Graphiti hybrid search
Returns gap_detected (bool), gap_summary, affected_sop_rule, governing_rule_violated, confidence (0.0-1.0), relevant_graph_nodes array, suggested_patch_text
gap_detected=true for the Meridian scenario when all three demo docs are ingested
confidence >= 0.75 for the demo scenario
Returns gap_detected=false with confidence=0 when graph is empty
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: Confidence = ratio of expected nodes found (3/3 = 1.0). suggested_patch_text is prose synthesized from graph context, not a Claude API call. Keep deterministic for demo reliability.

userStories.title: Add patch proposal endpoint to sidecar
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need a POST /patches/propose endpoint that generates a governed SOP patch with traceability from gap detection results.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /patches/propose endpoint exists
Accepts JSON with agent_id, sop_id, gap_detection_result (full response from /gaps/detect)
Calls Claude API (claude-sonnet-4-20250514) to generate patch_text
Has hardcoded FALLBACK_PATCH for Rule 3.2.1 Multi-Entity Approval Routing used if Claude API fails (try/catch confirmed)
Returns patch_id (uuid), patch_text, patch_rule_id (3.2.1), patch_location, confidence, diff (before/after), traceability object, status (staged)
Traceability block contains all three source documents
diff.before contains original Rule 3.2 text, diff.after contains Rule 3.2 + Rule 3.2.1
status is 'staged' for confidence >= 0.75
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: Store patch in-memory (dict keyed by patch_id). No new Convex table needed. Convex SOP versioning handles the canonical record.

userStories.title: Add patch approve/reject endpoints to sidecar
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need POST /patches/{patch_id}/approve and /patches/{patch_id}/reject endpoints to handle patch decisions.
acceptanceCriteria: POST /patches/{patch_id}/approve endpoint exists
Approve accepts JSON with agent_id, sop_id, approved_by
Approve returns status='approved', new_sop_version='v2.2', previous_version='v2.1', patch_id, approved_at (ISO timestamp)
POST /patches/{patch_id}/reject endpoint exists
Reject returns status='rejected', patch_id
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: On approve: return approval metadata only. The frontend handles Convex SOP versioning. On reject: mark patch as rejected in-memory.

userStories.title: Add demo state aggregation endpoint to sidecar
userStories.description: As a backend developer, I need a GET /demo/state endpoint that returns the full current demo state (ingested docs, graph nodes/edges, gap, patch) in one call for the frontend to consume.
acceptanceCriteria: GET /demo/state?agent_id={agent_id} endpoint exists
Returns documents_ingested array (source_name, document_type, entity_count, ingested_at)
Returns graph_node_count, graph_edge_count
Returns latest_gap (null if no gap detected yet)
Returns latest_patch (null if no patch proposed yet)
Returns graph_nodes array (node_id, entity_type, name, summary, source_document) sufficient for d3-force rendering
Returns graph_edges array (source_id, target_id, relationship_type, fact) sufficient for d3-force rendering
Responds in < 2 seconds
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: This is the 'god endpoint' the frontend polls for all demo state. Aggregates in-memory data from ingestion, gap detection, and patch proposal steps.

userStories.title: Fix Graphiti sidecar connection in KG tab
userStories.description: As a frontend developer, I need to fix the 'fetch failed' error in the Knowledge Graph tab so it correctly connects to the Graphiti sidecar.
acceptanceCriteria: KG tab no longer shows 'fetch failed' when sidecar is running on localhost:8000
Loading spinner shown during fetch
Graceful offline state if sidecar is down: 'Knowledge graph offline' with subtle reconnect indicator (not broken-looking)
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: Update src/lib/graphiti-client.ts and the KG tab component. Confirm sidecar URL (likely http://localhost:8000). Replace whatever hardcoded URL or env var is failing.

userStories.title: Build three-panel Knowledge Graph UI — graph canvas
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a d3-force graph visualization of knowledge graph nodes and edges so I can explore entity relationships.
acceptanceCriteria: Three-panel layout: graph canvas (60%), node detail (20%), signal feed (20%)
d3-force graph renders nodes and edges from GET /demo/state endpoint data
Node colors by entity type: SOP=#9CA896 (sage), GovernanceRule=#7CA8D8 (blue), AgentSignal=#D8B87C (amber), other=#505050 (muted)
Node sizes: degree-based sizing (min 6px radius, max 16px)
Hover node shows tooltip with entity name + type label in IBM Plex Mono uppercase
New node pulse animation: scale 1→1.3→1, 600ms when first appearing after ingestion
Layout stable on 1280px+ viewport
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: d3-force is already installed (@types/d3-force in devDependencies). Fetch from GET /demo/state. Panel 2 and 3 are separate stories.

userStories.title: Build KG node detail panel and signal feed panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click a graph node to see its details, and see a real-time signal feed of ingestion events.
acceptanceCriteria: Click node in graph canvas populates Panel 2 (node detail) with: entity type badge, entity name, summary, source document, connected nodes list
Panel 2 empty state: 'Select a node to inspect' in muted text
Panel 3 (signal feed) shows live feed of ingestion events and gap detection signals
Each signal card: rounded-[4px] border-l-2 bg-[#181818] p-3, border color matches entity type color
Signal feed updates via polling (every 3s) or Convex real-time subscription
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: Panel 2 data comes from node click. Panel 3 data comes from Convex agentDocuments or containerRuns table, or from /demo/state polling.

userStories.title: Documents tab — type selector and sidecar upload
userStories.description: As a user, I want to upload documents with a type selector (governance/sop/agent_log) and see them ingested into the knowledge graph with entity counts.
acceptanceCriteria: Document type selector before upload: dropdown or segmented control with options governance | sop | agent_log
Upload reads file as text and calls POST /ingest/document on the sidecar
On success: document card appears with type badge and entity count from response
Badge colors: governance=#7CA8D8 at 15% opacity, sop=#9CA896 at 15% opacity, agent_log=#D8B87C at 15% opacity
On error: red toast 'Ingestion failed — [error message]'
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: Current state: upload area + 'No documents uploaded yet' empty state. Add type selector and wire upload to sidecar instead of (or in addition to) existing Convex upload.

userStories.title: SOPs tab — active and staged SOP cards
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see active SOPs and staged patches in the SOPs tab so I can review and approve SOP changes.
acceptanceCriteria: SOPs tab shows two sections: Active and Staged
Active SOP card shows: [ACTIVE] badge (sage green), version (v2.1), title, last updated, rule count, arrow link
Staged SOP card appears after patch proposal: [STAGED] badge (amber), [PENDING APPROVAL] badge, version (v2.2 draft), proposed change count, trigger escalation ID
Staged card has [Reject] and [Review & Approve →] buttons
'Review & Approve →' navigates to /dashboard/[agentId]/patch/[sopId]
After approval, card updates from staged to [ACTIVE] v2.2
Data sourced from Convex sops and sopVersions tables
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: Current state: 'No SOPs for this agent yet.' empty state. On approve from diff page: Convex mutation archives current version, creates new active sopVersions record with v2.2 content.

userStories.title: SOP Diff page — left panel with GitHub-style diff renderer
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see the proposed SOP patch as a GitHub-style red/green diff so I can review exactly what changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: /dashboard/[agentId]/patch/[sopId] exists
Two-column layout: SOP diff (left, 55%) and traceability (right, 45%)

Diff renders with: removed lines bg-[#2d1515] border-l-4 border-[#D87C7C] text-[#D87C7C], added lines bg-[#152d1a] border-l-4 border-[#9CA896] text-[#9CA896], context lines bg-[#181818] text-[#707070]
Line numbers in IBM Plex Mono, muted
Version labels below diff: CURRENT v2.1 → PROPOSED v2.2
Approve and Reject buttons below version labels, monospace uppercase full-width
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: Diff data comes from patch proposal response (diff.before / diff.after). Traceability panel is a separate story.

userStories.title: SOP Diff page — traceability panel with accordion panels
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see why a patch was proposed via three traceability accordion panels (Agent Signal, Governance Rule, SOP Gap) with expand-to-source capability.
acceptanceCriteria: Right panel header: 'WHY THIS PATCH?' in IBM Plex Mono uppercase
Three accordion panels all expanded by default: Agent Signal (RULE_CONFLICT), Governance Rule (G-006), SOP Gap (Rule 3.2)
Each panel shows structured metadata matching the demo scenario data
'Expand to source' toggle on each panel shows raw source text excerpt in bg-[#0F0F0F] font-mono text-sm text-[#A0A0A0] p-4 rounded-[4px]
Traceability data sourced from patch proposal response traceability object
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: All three panels must show correct data for the Meridian demo scenario. Agent Signal panel shows escalation_id, deal_id, entities, timestamp. Governance panel shows rule_id, enforcement, requirement. SOP Gap panel shows current_rule, gap description, patch.

userStories.title: SOP Diff page — approve/reject actions with Convex versioning
userStories.description: As a user, I want to approve or reject a patch from the diff page, with approve creating a new SOP version in Convex.
acceptanceCriteria: Approve button calls POST /patches/{patch_id}/approve on sidecar
On approve success: Convex mutation archives current active version, creates new sopVersions record with v2.2 content
Success state: sage green banner 'Patch approved. SOP updated to v2.2.'
Navigates back to SOPs tab after 2 seconds
Reject button calls POST /patches/{patch_id}/reject on sidecar
On reject: brief 'Patch rejected.' message, navigates back to SOPs tab
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: Convex mutations needed: mark current sop as archived/deprecated, create new sopVersions record. Use existing sops and sopVersions tables from schema.

userStories.title: Live SOP Test page — side-by-side Adela vs Claude MD comparison
userStories.description: As a user, I want to run a scenario through both Adela and raw Claude Memory/MD side-by-side to see why Adela is better for traceability.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: /dashboard/[agentId]/sop-test exists
Scenario textarea pre-filled with Meridian co-signatory scenario text
'Run Test' button triggers two Claude API calls simultaneously
Left panel (Adela): system prompt instructs structured governed SOP response with citations and version tracking
Right panel (Claude MD): system prompt is generic helpful assistant with pasted SOP markdown
Loading states shown while calls are in flight
Capability comparison chips below each panel: gap detection (✓/✗), governance referenced (✓/✗), patch proposed (✓/✗), audit trail (✓/✗)
Hardcoded fallback responses used if Claude API calls fail (try/catch for both)
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: Claude calls made from API route (not client-side). Left panel uses structured Adela system prompt. Right panel uses generic assistant prompt. Fallback text defined in PRD Section 6.2.

userStories.title: Benchmark page — static 9/10 vs 4/10 comparison
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a static benchmark page comparing Adela (9/10) vs Claude Memory/MD (4/10) on traceability-dependent scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: Route: /dashboard/benchmark exists
Page is fully static — no API calls, no loading states
Two large score cards: Adela 9/10 (sage green accent) and Claude MD 4/10 (muted/gray)
Progress bars: Adela 90% filled, Claude MD 40% filled
Table of 10 scenarios with: scenario description, Adela result (✓ sage/✗ red), Claude MD result (✓/✗), failure chips
Failure chips: MULTI-SIGNAL, GOVERNANCE-DEPENDENT, VERSION-AWARENESS, GRAPH-TRAVERSAL in colored monospace badges
Alternating row background: #181818 / #121212
Navigable from sidebar or top nav (add 'Benchmark' link)
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: All data is hardcoded. 10 scenarios exactly as defined in PRD Section 7. Score: Adela passes scenarios 1-10 except one partial (9/10). Claude MD passes scenarios 5, 9 and two partials (4/10).

userStories.title: Add demo navigation links to dashboard sidebar
userStories.description: As a user, I want to navigate to the new demo pages (Benchmark, Live SOP Test) from the dashboard sidebar/nav.
acceptanceCriteria: Benchmark link added to dashboard sidebar or navigation
Live SOP Test link added (if not already accessible from SOPs tab)
Links navigate correctly to /dashboard/benchmark and /dashboard/[agentId]/sop-test
Active state styling matches existing nav pattern
Typecheck/lint passes
userStories.notes: Check existing dashboard layout.tsx for nav pattern. Add links following existing sidebar component structure.

scope: No breaking changes to existing routes — current dashboard shell with four tabs must continue to work
Hardcoded fallbacks required on all Claude API calls (Live SOP Test, Patch Proposal)
Demo data is fixed — do not change scenario (deal ID, entity names, escalation ID, SOP rule numbers)
No new Graphiti infrastructure — use existing ENTITY_TYPES and EDGE_TYPES dicts
No new Convex tables — use existing sops, sopVersions, agentDocuments, containerRuns
Sidecar state is in-memory for the demo — patch proposals and gap detection results don't need Convex persistence

performance.allEndpoints: < 2 seconds response time in happy path

security.auth: Clerk handles all auth. Sidecar endpoints are internal-only (no Clerk auth needed on sidecar).
security.secrets: ANTHROPIC_API_KEY for Claude calls in Live SOP Test and patch generation. All server-side only.

nonGoals: Mobile responsive design for demo pages
